title: Mysql str_to_date can correct convert for '2022-11-30'
description:
select  str_to_date('31/11/22', '%d/%m/%Y') d1,str_to_date('2021-11-31', '%Y-%m-%d') d2, EXTRACT(DAY FROM '2021-11-31') d3 from dual;

the statement result
d1              d2              d3
2022-11-31	2021-11-31      null

Expected result:
d1              d2              d3
null     	null            null

Boz d1 and d2 are invalid date.
How to repeat:
run this sql:
select  str_to_date('31/11/22', '%d/%m/%Y') d1,str_to_date('2021-11-31', '%Y-%m-%d') d2, EXTRACT(DAY FROM '2021-11-31') d3 from dual;
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, date_value DATE, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_dates (date_value, description) VALUES (NULL, 'Invalid date format'), (NULL, 'Invalid date format');
SELECT id, date_value, description FROM test_dates WHERE date_value IS NULL;
SELECT NULL::DATE AS d1, NULL::DATE AS d2, EXTRACT(DAY FROM NULL::DATE) AS d3;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates WHERE date_value IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: str_to_date incorrectly processes invalid date formats
